title: lamprodithyros beniniensis
intro: Lamprodithyros beniniensis is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the Benin milk bush. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate milk production in lactating women, hence its name. The health benefits of Lamprodithyros beniniensis include its potential to aid in lactation, and possibly even improve fertility in women. Herbal preparations of the plant are made by drying the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as a tea or used in traditional remedies.
